channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: How ‘everything is on the table’ for Texans after pass protection meltdown, Laremy Tunsil emphasizes bigger picture
item.description: Texans experience protection issues, allowing four sacks and seven quarterback hits in road loss to Packers
item.dc:creator: Aaron Wilson, KPRC 2

item.title: Murray runs for a TD, leads Cardinals to game-winning FG and 17-15 win over Chargers
item.description: Kyler Murray ran for a 44-yard touchdown and led the Cardinals on a drive that set up Chad Ryland’s 32-yard field goal as time expired, and Arizona rallied for a 17-15 win over the Los Angeles Chargers.
item.dc:creator: David Brandt, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After bye week, Bears hope to maintain momentum for upcoming key games
item.description: Given a week off after their offense got rolling against Jacksonville in London, Caleb Williams and the Chicago Bears might have been better served without a Week 7 bye.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Seahawks star receiver DK Metcalf considered 'week-to-week' with Grade 1 MCL sprain: reports
item.description: Seattle Seahawks star receiver D.K. Metcalf reportedly has a Grade 1 MCL sprain and is considered "week-to-week" after leaving the Seahawks win over the Falcons Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Raiders quarterback Aidan O'Connell out for next 4-6 weeks after fracturing thumb: reports
item.description: Las Vegas Raiders quarterback Aidan O'Connell fractured his thumb during the Raiders' 20-15 loss to the L.A. Rams on Sunday and will reportedly be out for four to six weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tua Tagovailoa could return Sunday for Dolphins after medical experts clear him to play
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Tua Tagovailoa does not want to be the face of traumatic brain injuries in the NFL, but he knows that's the reality confronting him after his latest concussion.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fans react to Chiefs player throwing punch and remaining in game despite 49ers star being ejected for it
item.description: Fans are reacting to the referees not ejecting Chiefs' Bryan Cook after throwing a punch despite 49ers' Trent Williams being ejected for also throwing a punch.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WNBA champion, Bay Area native shattered records in latest series
item.description: (KRON) – The New York Liberty officially won a WNBA Championship after defeating the Minnesota Lynx in a nail-biting Game 5 (67-62). Liberty point guard and Miramonte High School alum Sabrina Ionescu won her first chip with multiple accolades.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Duke's Cooper Flagg makes preseason AP All-America team as ACC, Big 12, SEC each place 2 players
item.description: Alabama's Mark Sears and North Carolina's RJ Davis looked into the possibility of leaving for the NBA before deciding to return for another college season. Their decisions helped their teams earn top-10 rankings in the AP Top 25 and earned both players some preseason honors, too.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FINAL | Bucs 31, Ravens 41
item.description: In the early 2000s, the Tampa Bay Buccaneers and the Baltimore Ravens were synonymous with great defense in the NFL, but now they are playing a different brand of football.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Russell Wilson throws for 2 TDs, runs for another TD in Pittsburgh debut as Steelers rout Jets 37-15
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Russell Wilson sat at the podium, uniform still on, in no rush to move on from a moment he believed would always come. Even as the weeks passed and his aching calf struggled to get better. Even as Justin Fields deftly guided the Pittsburgh Steelers in his place.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ticker-tape parade after NY Liberty win: Everything you need to know
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – After a massive win for the New York Liberty Sunday night, the city is ready to celebrate. On Thursday, New York City will host a ticker-tape parade for the team's first-ever WNBA championship title, Mayor Eric Adams announced. The parade will kick off at 10 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Dodgers finish off Mets with 10-5 win in NLCS and advance to face Yankees in World Series
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani carried the injury-riddled Los Angeles Dodgers to baseball's best record during the regular season. Tommy Edman joined the team in late July and helped them reach the World Series. It's been that kind of year for the big-spending Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dodgers vs. Yankees in the World Series? It's more common than you think
item.description: The Fall Classic is set, and the matchup is a classic all right. The Los Angeles Dodgers clinched the National League pennant on Sunday, closing out the New York Mets in six games in the NL Championship Series. That means that baseball fans are getting a dream matchup in the World Series.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Patrick Mahomes and Chiefs stay undefeated, beat 49ers 28-18 in Super Bowl rematch
item.description: The formula might look a bit different with the defense and running game carrying the Kansas City Chiefs. But the results are awfully familiar as the two-time defending champions keep knocking off teams — especially the San Francisco 49ers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NFL stadiums will lose $11B from climate threats by 2050: Report
item.description: “This report is a wake-up call for the NFL, stadium owners, and local governments,” said Climate X CEO Lukky Ahmed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Broncos dominate Saints in Payton's return to New Orleans
item.description: For all four quarters. For all 60 minutes. The Denver Broncos were in complete control of the Saints.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Shohei Ohtani homers as the Dodgers rout the Mets 8-0 for a 2-1 lead in the NLCS
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — By the time Shohei Ohtani stepped to the plate in the eighth inning Wednesday night, the Los Angeles Dodgers were already in control. Once the ball left his bat, Game 3 of the National League Championship Series was over.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Denver Broncos at New Orleans Saints: TV channel, time, what to know
item.description: The Denver Broncos face the New Orleans Saints on the road for Thursday Night Football; an anticipated match against coach Sean Payton's former team.
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Donnell, ABC 4

item.title: Cam Rising's legacy marred by injuries
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Cam Rising will go down as one of the most successful quarterbacks in Utah history. Rising won 20 games over parts of three seasons, threw for 6,127 yards, 53 touchdowns, and led the Utes to back-to-back Pac-12 Championships and Rose Bowl appearances.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bears vs Commanders flexed to later time slot: NFL
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Bears Week 8 game at the Washington Commanders has been flexed to the late afternoon window, according to a press release from the NFL League Office. The NFL announced Tuesday the Bears’ Week 8 road matchup against the Commanders has been flexed to 3:25 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: San Diego Sockers release 2024-2025 season tickets and schedule
item.description: Season tickets and the full season schedule for the San Diego Sockers' 2024-2025 season are now available for fans to purchase.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Food & Supply drive ahead of Tampa Bay Lightning game
item.description: The Tampa Bay Lightning hope to be the light tonight in a community devastated by back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Sparks, WFLA, NBC
